rows.description: Computus Geo. Payther Rectoris a secundo die Novemb. 1708 ad eundem diem 1709.
rows.notes: Page title / header across the top. Very clear handwriting and location; no monetary columns present.
Section-heading style phrase just under the title. Text is legible; no amounts associated on the same line.

Unusual pound notation '-AA' is clearly written in the pounds column (user guidance confirms to extract literally). Pence shown as '1d' — per page guidance 'd' interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Column alignment referenced to total row; digits clearly aligned.
Section header introducing arrears. No amounts on this line.
Clear numeric trio at right: '60 00 00'. Alignment with total row is unambiguous.
Numbers read as '59 19 9' and align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns defined by the total row. Handwriting of the pence is slightly cramped but clearly '9'.
Clear '20 00 00' at right; alignment consistent with column guides.
Clear '58 00 00' in the rightmost columns; very legible.
Hand reads as 'Cuddlington Rry pro mich' (the middle word is abbreviated/uncertain). Numeric '10 00 00' is clear. Slight uncertainty in transcription of the place name.
Numbers appear as '03 08 08'. Column alignment clear; handwriting of abbreviation in description is moderately unclear (R?y).

This line is present as a named entry (place + sesquianno) but I see no associated amounts on the same line. Handwriting of place name is moderately ambiguous; left as an amount-less entry (likely a grouped sub-item).
Short place-name line with no amounts; appears to be a descriptive/structural label.
Purely descriptive line with no amounts adjacent. The words are legible.
Clear small-figure entry at right read as '00 02 06'. Alignment with total row consistent.

Right-side figures appear as '04 5 3' but the pence digit and any trailing fraction are slightly cramped; chosen reading is '3' pence with no fraction. Column alignment used to separate pounds/shillings/pence.
Figures read as '03 3 3d'. The 'd' after the pence is visible and per page-specific guidance is interpreted as a half-penny (0.5). Alignment with column lines checked against total row.
Numbers read as '08 12 3d' (pence followed by 'd' halfpenny). Shillings '12' is valid. The halfpenny marker is faint but consistent with similar marks elsewhere.
Long descriptive entry; I do not see clear amounts on the same line. Some of the slavery of ink and abbreviations make the text moderately uncertain.
Name + term present with no visible amounts adjacent. Handwriting of name is legible; absence of numbers is clear.
Descriptive entry with no amounts on this line. Legible heading but no numerical trio visible.
Entry present without adjacent amounts. Text clarity moderate; column alignment shows no numbers here.
Clear numeric trio '00 15 0' to the right of the description. Alignment is consistent and digits are legible.
Numbers read as '01 00 00'. Well aligned to column guides; handwriting clear.
Name present but no visible amounts on same line. Slight ambiguity in reading the name but context suggests it's an entry without amounts.
Numerics to right appear as '04 18 7' (there are additional faint marks nearby but the core trio is readable). Chosen to omit any fraction due to ambiguity of the faint trailing mark.
Main heading 'Tintinhull' with a large brace indicating grouped subentries below (brace visible). No amounts on the heading itself.
Sub-entry under Tintinhull; numerics appear near the right as '02 12 10'. Pence '10' is within valid range. The tens-digit pence is slightly cramped but alignment with columns is consistent.
Appears as '06 7 8q' where the trailing 'q' is visible after the pence and per page guidance indicates a farthing (0.25). Alignment checked against the total row. The 'q' is faint but recognizable.
Right-side numbers read '04 17 2'. The place name has an uncommon spelling but the monetary figures are clear.
Numeric trio reads '10 1 9' (shillings single-digit). Column alignment consistent; text of place name moderately legible.
Right figures read '08 3 6'. The place name is somewhat abbreviated (I read 'Brewlcuge'); monetary values are clearly placed and legible.
Descriptive line with no visible amounts on the same row. Handwriting of the description is moderately legible.
Entry text present but no adjacent monetary figures. The place-name spelling is not fully certain; treated as an amount-less entry.
Descriptive / grouped entry with no amounts visible on the same line. Handwriting of the phrase is moderately unclear but legible enough to record.
Numeric '04 00 00' is present and clear at the right; name is legible too.

Line reads 'Yardington mich' with figures '05 00 00' visible. IMPORTANT: this row is struck-through (heavily crossed out). Transcribed full text and amounts per instruction; the strike-through makes the handwriting and numbers less legible, reducing confidence.

Line reads roughly 'Jue exstrents & anno' with numeric trio resembling '05 07 02'. This row is struck-through (crossed out). The strike-through obscures some letters and digits; chosen reading follows visible strokes and alignment with other rows but is moderately uncertain. Marked as deleted/struck-through per page-specific guidance.
Numeric trio at right reads '00 08 02'. Text 'Warner & Biennio' is legible and aligned with the numbers. Slight waviness in ink but digits clear.
A horizontal rule is present above the final total. Included as a visual/structural row (no amounts).
Final total on the page read as '322 19 9'. This row was used as the primary alignment reference for the £ / s / d columns; digits are crisp and align well with other rows.
